beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee and toast at home
beats.reasoning: Typical morning routine; yesterday's low energy (45/100) and quiet evening suggest a calm, deliberate start. Grey drizzly weather makes a hot coffee feel essential.

beats.activity: Walk to work downtown, sheltering under umbrella in steady rain
beats.reasoning: Fatima walks most days; the rain doesn't stop her, just slows her slightly. SR-520 closure is irrelevant since she doesn't cross the lake. Generous 30-min buffer for wet conditions.

beats.activity: Teach science classes and grade papers during prep period
beats.reasoning: Standard teaching day; rain continues steadily through noon, so she stays indoors. No reason to leave campus during the morning.

beats.activity: Lunch at Mighty O Donuts, grab a bagel, brief people-watch
beats.reasoning: Fatima has strong bagel opinions and visited yesterday; rain is ending by 1pm, so a 15-min walk from downtown is comfortable. Repeating a favorite spot fits her introverted, routine-oriented nature.

beats.activity: Walk back to school after lunch, rain tapering off
beats.reasoning: Return to work; rain is mostly done by 2pm. 35-min walk back allows time to decompress before afternoon classes.

beats.activity: Teach final classes and wrap up marking
beats.reasoning: Standard afternoon teaching block; weather has cleared by now, but she stays at school to finish administrative work.

beats.activity: Walk home to South Lake Union, clearing skies
beats.reasoning: Afternoon is dry; 40-min walk aligns with her preference to walk most days. No detours needed since SR-520 doesn't affect pedestrians.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up brewing supplies and fresh yeast at local homebrew shop
beats.reasoning: Fatima is actively engaged in home brewing; a Tuesday afternoon errand in nearby Fremont fits her frugal, independent routine. Stays out of the house but close to home.
Short 20-min walk from Fremont back to South Lake Union, carrying small supplies.

beats.activity: Home brewing session: sanitize equipment, prepare mash, measure grains, start fermentation
beats.reasoning: Continuation of yesterday's meditative brewing project; low energy from yesterday makes staying home appealing. Rain has stopped, so perfect indoor focus time.

beats.activity: Make simple dinner, eat at home, sort through film photos on laptop
beats.reasoning: Introverted, frugal routine; cooking at home and reviewing photography fits her personality. Quiet evening mirrors yesterday's mood.

diary:
Woke to rain hammering the windows—one of those Seattle days where you know the commute will be a slog. Coffee and toast helped. The walk downtown was actually peaceful under my umbrella, even if my shoes got soaked. Classes felt productive; the kids were surprisingly engaged during the photosynthesis unit. Grabbed a bagel at Mighty O after and watched the lunch crowd blur past. By the time I headed back, the rain was already letting up.

After school I made a beeline for Fremont to pick up supplies for a new batch—been craving a good IPA project. The clearing skies felt like permission. Spent the evening sanitizing equipment and measuring grains, that meditative rhythm of prep work. There's something grounding about it, especially after a teaching day. Threw together a simple dinner and sorted through some old film photos on my laptop. Small, full day.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure were background noise—I walk to work anyway, so traffic doesn't touch me. What pulled me through was structure: teaching energized me, then the shift to the personal project afterward balanced it out. I chose the homebrew supply run deliberately; I'd been putting it off, and the clearing skies felt like momentum. The film photos at night were pure decompression—something tactile and unhurried after hours of student questions. My mood lifted as the day progressed, maybe because the weather improved, maybe because I moved from obligation (teaching) into choice (brewing). Both matter.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
